what: A worked example on simulated plots
title: Compare embedding methods on your own data
text: simulation draws species presence smooth latent niches, species nearby niches co-occur carry cover tracks central plot niche. Run comparison. co-occurrence metrics need x:
code: sim_plots <- function(M = 500, S = 60, seed = 1) {   set.seed(seed)   mu  <- matrix(rnorm(S * 2), S, 2)   env <- matrix(rnorm(M * 2), M, 2)   rows <- lapply(seq_len(M), function(p) {     d2   <- rowSums((mu - matrix(env[p, ], S, 2, byrow = TRUE))^2)     prob <- exp(-d2 / 2)     present <- which(runif(S) < prob)     if (length(present) < 2) present <- order(prob, decreasing = TRUE)[1:2]     data.frame(plot = paste0("p", p), species = paste0("sp", present),                cover = round(100 * prob[present] / max(prob[present]), 1))   })   do.call(rbind, rows) }  df <- sim_plots(M = 500, S = 60) x  <- specvec(df, "plot", "species", abundance = "cover") x #> <specvec_data> plots=500  species=60 #>   presence: nnz=10352  density=34.5067% #>   abundance: yes (cover_scale=percent)  duplicates=max b <- compare_embeddings(   x,   methods = c("ca", "pmi", "abund_pmi"),   metrics = c("cooc_ppmi", "cooc_raw", "link_auc"),   dim = 16, seeds = 1:3, min_occurrence = 3 ) b #> <specvec_benchmark> plots=500  species=60  dim=16  seeds=1,2,3 #>   methods: ca, pmi, abund_pmi #>  #>   method            cooc_ppmi         cooc_raw #>   abund_pmi      0.827+-0.028     0.652+-0.022 #>   pmi            0.820+-0.028     0.589+-0.029 #>   ca             0.784+-0.031     0.502+-0.023 #>  #>   verdict vs 'ca' (beats on cooc_raw & link_auc by >2 pooled SDs): #>     reference 'ca' absent; ordering only.

title: Compare embedding methods on your own data
text: method fit training plots scored pairs: cooc_raw: Spearman correlation embedding’s pair scores held-raw co-occurrence counts. neutral co-occurrence metric. link_auc: well pair scores rank co-occurring pairs pairs never co-occur either split. AUC 0.5 chance. cooc_ppmi: Spearman held-PPMI. PMI methods optimise PPMI-style objective, reported reference. verdict applies fixed rule decided looking: method beats reference (ca default) exceeds neutral metrics (cooc_raw link_auc) two pooled standard deviations. latent-niche simulation unimodal-gradient regime correspondence analysis built , CA scores well . abundance-weighted advantage sets abund_pmi package default measured continental vegetation data 10,000 200,000 plots, co-occurrence sparse species set large.

title: Compare embedding methods on your own data
text: Two metrics activate supply labels. EUNIS habitat plot-level: pass label column carried specvec object add "eunis" metrics. Traits species-level: pass species--trait matrix traits add "trait". score k-nearest-neighbour recovery skip cleanly inputs absent.
code: b <- compare_embeddings(   x,   methods = c("ca", "pmi", "abund_pmi", "glove"),   metrics = c("cooc_raw", "link_auc", "eunis", "trait"),   labels  = "habitat",     # a label column on `x`   traits  = trait_matrix,  # rows named by species   dim = 64, seeds = 1:3 )

title: Compare embedding methods on your own data
text: compare_embeddings() takes specvec object, bake-runs vegetation database export. European Vegetation Archive (EVA) species table plot id, taxon name, cover column becomes specvec like : package carries vegetation data ; comparison meant run .
code: library(data.table) sp <- fread("species_export.csv") sp <- sp[rank == "species"]                       # taxonomic scope is the caller's  x <- specvec(sp, plot = "PlotObservationID", species = "taxon",              abundance = "cover", labels = "Eunis_lvl2")  compare_embeddings(x, dim = 64, seeds = 1:3, min_occurrence = 5)

title: Methods: from plots to embeddings
text: specvec() reads long table builds two sparse matrices sorted species index: presence P (plot species, 0/1) , cover supplied, COV (plot species, cover [0, 1]). Duplicate plot-species rows aggregated duplicates rule ("max" default), ordinal cover scales convert cover_scale. raw co-occurrence count two species number plots holding , crossprod(P). diagonal species’ occurrence count.
code: df <- data.frame(   plot    = c("p1","p1","p2","p2","p3","p3","p4","p4"),   species = c("A","B","A","B","A","C","B","C"),   cover   = c(80, 20, 50, 50, 60, 40, 40, 60) ) x <- specvec(df, "plot", "species", abundance = "cover") x #> <specvec_data> plots=4  species=3 #>   presence: nnz=8  density=66.6667% #>   abundance: yes (cover_scale=percent)  duplicates=max

title: Methods: from plots to embeddings
text: AbundPMI pointwise mutual information computed cover-weighted co-occurrence operator. Writing W = sqrt(COV) element-wise: co-occurring plot contributes geometric mean two covers, pair two abundant species weighs pair one trace. marginal f[] total cover species , cover-weighted analogue occurrence frequency. positive PMI kept (PPMI), diagonal excluded. operator symmetric, factorization eigendecomposition scaled square roots positive eigenvalues. Plain PMI path W = P, binary presence matrix. AbundPMI exactly PMI presence replaced sqrt(COV), makes abundance effect clean /B comparison. dataset cover column, abund_pmi falls back presence PMI records object. cover-weighted operator also reachable directly cooc_matrix(x, "abundance_pmi").
code: A[a, b] = sum_p sqrt(cover[p, a] * cover[p, b])   # = t(W) %*% W, off-diagonal f[a]    = A[a, a] = sum_p cover[p, a]             # cover mass of species a PPMI[a, b] = max( log( A[a, b] * n_plots / (f[a] * f[b]) ), 0 )   for a != b emb        = V_k %*% diag(sqrt(Lambda_k))         # top-k eigenpairs of PPMI emb <- species_embedding(x, method = "abund_pmi", dim = 2, min_occurrence = 1) emb #> <specvec_embedding> method=abund_pmi  dim=2  species=3 #>   weighting=abundance_pmi  factorization=eigen #>   kept 3/3 species (min_occurrence=1, min_cooccurrence=1)  plots=4 species_similarity(emb, "A", "B") #> [1] 1

title: Methods: from plots to embeddings
text: ca correspondence analysis: singular value decomposition chi-square residuals plot--species contingency table. SpecVec applies matrix-vector products, dense contingency table never formed. pmi presence version AbundPMI . glove GloVe objective-factorizer text2vec (optional dependency); brings weighted least-squares loss bias terms, reads species vectors sum two weight matrices.
code: specvec_methods() #> [1] "abund_pmi" "ca"        "glove"     "pmi"

title: Methods: from plots to embeddings
text: min_occurrence drops species seen fewer many plots operator built, since singletons give unstable vectors. min_cooccurrence prunes rare species pairs. recorded fitted object. Eigen SVD vectors unique sign. SpecVec fixes sign embedding column largest-magnitude entry positive, deterministic leaves dot products cosines unchanged. Two fits data return identical matrices.

title: Methods: from plots to embeddings
text: community embedding places plot species space pooling vectors species contains: weights = "cover" (default cover present) weights cover; weights = "presence" 0/1, giving plain mean present species’ vectors. community_similarity() community_novelty() operate plot vectors.
code: U[p, ] = sum_s w[p, s] * V[s, ] / sum_s w[p, s] comm <- community_embedding(x, embedding = emb, weights = "cover") comm #> <specvec_community> plots=4  dim=2  pooling=cover #>   from: method=abund_pmi  weighting=abundance_pmi  factorization=eigen

title: Methods: from plots to embeddings
text: compare_embeddings() scores methods one protocol decides winner rule fixed results seen: method beats reference exceeds neutral co-occurrence metrics (held-raw Spearman link-prediction AUC) two pooled standard deviations. companion vignette vignette("specvec-benchmark") runs . continental vegetation data verdict selects abund_pmi, lead correspondence analysis grows number plots.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gilles Colling. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Colling G (2026). specvec: Abundance-Aware Species Community Embeddings. R package version 0.2.0, https://github.com/gcol33/specvec.
code: @Manual{,   title = {specvec: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings},   author = {Gilles Colling},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://github.com/gcol33/specvec}, }

title: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings
text: SpecVec learns abundance-aware species community embeddings ecological plot data. vegetation plot records species grow together much cover one . SpecVec turns co-occurrence dense vectors: species share habitat sit close together, every plot becomes point space. Cover enters weighting, dominant species carries weight trace occurrence.

title: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("gcol33/specvec")

title: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings
text: specvec() takes long table one row per plot-species record. Cover optional read percent default; proportion Braun-Blanquet scales built (cover_scale =).
code: library(specvec)  ## a long plot x species table, one row per record data <- data.frame(   plot    = c("p1","p1","p2","p2","p3","p3","p4","p4"),   species = c("Acer campestre","Sambucus nigra",               "Acer campestre","Prunus avium",               "Sambucus nigra","Prunus avium",               "Acer campestre","Sambucus nigra"),   cover   = c(40, 10, 55, 20, 30, 60, 25, 15) )  x    <- specvec(data, plot = "plot", species = "species", abundance = "cover") emb  <- species_embedding(x, dim = 2, min_occurrence = 1)  # abund_pmi by default nearest_species(emb, "Acer campestre") comm <- community_embedding(x, embedding = emb)            # one vector per plot

title: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings
text: Fitted 10,000-plot European vegetation sample (EVA), nearest neighbours three established neophytes recover communities one grows . Robinia pseudoacacia nitrogen-fixing tree dry, nutrient-rich woodland edges, neighbours hedgerow ruderal-forest species follow : Solidago gigantea Impatiens glandulifera two riverbank invaders, land nearly floodplain neighbourhood native willows, co-invading neophytes, tall riparian herbs: dry-edge tree two riparian herbs occupy distinct regions space, beside native co-invading species actually grows . vignette("specvec-benchmark") shows build specvec object EVA export reproduce .
code: nearest_species(emb, "Robinia pseudoacacia")                 species  similarity      Euonymus europaeus       0.770          Sambucus nigra       0.715            Prunus avium       0.710          Acer campestre       0.710           Rubus caesius       0.705 Solidago gigantea                Impatiens glandulifera    Humulus lupulus     0.838        Carduus crispus     0.857    Acer negundo        0.810        Salix euxina        0.852    Salix alba          0.801        Salix alba          0.839    Echinocystis lobata 0.778        Humulus lupulus     0.823    Solidago canadensis 0.772        Echinocystis lobata 0.807

title: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings
text: species_embedding() community_embedding() share one engine: co-occurrence operator paired factorization. default abund_pmi abundance-weighted PMI. Correspondence analysis (ca), plain PMI (pmi), glove reach call method registry, adding method registration. compare_embeddings() scores methods data one fair protocol (held-co-occurrence, link prediction, habitat recovery) reports method wins: See vignette("specvec-methods") definitions vignette("specvec-benchmark") run comparison.
code: compare_embeddings(x, methods = c("ca", "pmi", "abund_pmi", "glove"))

title: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings
text: v0.1 ships species community embeddings, neighbour novelty queries, benchmark. Temporal embeddings alien-integration trajectories roadmap. core methods install Matrix RSpectra alone; text2vec needed GloVe method.

title: Tidy a species trajectory — as.data.frame.specvec_trajectory
text: Long--wide data frame one row per focal-species--window cell: window label center, support (plots backing cell), dim coordinate columns d1..dD.

title: Tidy a species trajectory — as.data.frame.specvec_trajectory
code: # S3 method for class 'specvec_trajectory' as.data.frame(x, ..., na.rm = FALSE)

title: Tidy a species trajectory — as.data.frame.specvec_trajectory
text: x specvec_trajectory. ... Unused. na.rm Drop cells co-occurrence (-NA coordinates).

title: Tidy a species trajectory — as.data.frame.specvec_trajectory
text: data frame.

title: Community embedding — community_embedding
text: Place plot species embedding space pooling vectors species contains. readout uniform across methods (pooled species vectors), plot embeddings stay comparable. weights = "cover" pools cover (abundance moat plot level); weights = "presence" plain mean present species vectors.

title: Community embedding — community_embedding
code: community_embedding(   x,   method = "abund_pmi",   dim = 64L,   weights = c("cover", "presence"),   embedding = NULL,   normalize = FALSE,   time = NULL,   min_occurrence = 5L,   min_cooccurrence = 1L,   ... )

title: Community embedding — community_embedding
text: x specvec_data object. method Embedding method (see specvec_methods()); ignored embedding supplied. dim Embedding dimension; ignored embedding supplied. weights "cover" (default cover present) "presence". embedding Optional pre-fitted specvec_embedding pool, instead fitting one . normalize TRUE, L2-normalize plot vector pooling. time Optional time window (see species_embedding()): pool (, fitting, train ) plots window. NULL (default) uses plots. per-window communities one shared frame, use community_trajectory(). min_occurrence, min_cooccurrence Passed species_embedding() fitting. ... Passed species_embedding().

title: Community embedding — community_embedding
text: specvec_community: plot x dim matrix U (plot row names) plus pooling provenance metadata.

title: Community embedding — community_embedding
code: df <- data.frame(plot = rep(paste0("p", 1:8), each = 2),   species = c("A","B","A","B","A","C","B","C","A","B","B","C","A","C","A","B"),   cover = c(80,20,50,50,60,40,50,50,70,30,40,60,90,10,55,45)) x <- specvec(df, "plot", "species", abundance = "cover") community_embedding(x, method = "abund_pmi", dim = 3, min_occurrence = 1)

title: Community novelty — community_novelty
text: Per-plot novelty: mean distance k nearest reference communities. plot far everything reference set scores high.

title: Community novelty — community_novelty
code: community_novelty(object, reference, k = 5L)

title: Community novelty — community_novelty
text: object specvec_community (plots score). reference specvec_community matrix reference communities. k Number nearest reference communities average .

title: Community novelty — community_novelty
text: named numeric vector novelty per plot.

title: Community similarity — community_similarity
text: Pairwise similarity among plot embeddings, plot reference set.

title: Community similarity — community_similarity
code: community_similarity(   object,   reference = NULL,   metric = c("cosine", "euclidean") )

title: Community similarity — community_similarity
text: object specvec_community. reference Optional specvec_community matrix compare ; NULL, compares object's plots . metric "cosine" (default) returns similarity matrix; "euclidean" returns distance matrix.

title: Community similarity — community_similarity
text: matrix object rows reference rows.

what: Community trajectory and novelty over time — community_trajectory
title: Community trajectory and novelty over time — community_trajectory
text: Embed time window's communities one fixed frame score novel relative reference window. species frame fitted plots, per-window community embeddings live space novelty comparable across time (temporal counterpart community_novelty()).

title: Community trajectory and novelty over time — community_trajectory
code: community_trajectory(   x,   by = NULL,   reference = NULL,   method = "abund_pmi",   dim = 64L,   weights = c("cover", "presence"),   normalize = FALSE,   k = 5L,   min_occurrence = 5L,   min_cooccurrence = 1L,   frame_embedding = NULL,   ... )

title: Community trajectory and novelty over time — community_trajectory
text: x specvec_data object built time column. Window definition (see species_trajectory()). reference Reference window novelty measured : NULL (default) uses first window, window label index picks one, specvec_community/matrix supplies external baseline. method, dim, min_occurrence, min_cooccurrence Frame embedding controls. weights "cover" (default present) "presence" pooling. normalize TRUE, L2-normalize community vector scoring. k Neighbours averaged novelty distance. frame_embedding Optional pre-fitted frame embedding reuse. ... Passed species_embedding() fitting frame.

title: Community trajectory and novelty over time — community_trajectory
text: specvec_community_trajectory: communities (per-window plot x dim matrices shared frame), novelty table (window, center, n_plots, mean median per-plot novelty vs reference), fixed frame, resolved reference label.

title: Community trajectory and novelty over time — community_trajectory
code: set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   plot = rep(paste0("p", 1:90), each = 3),   species = sample(paste0("s", 1:15), 270, replace = TRUE),   decade = rep(c(1990, 2000, 2010), each = 90) ) x <- specvec(df, "plot", "species", time = "decade") community_trajectory(x, dim = 4, min_occurrence = 1, k = 3)

title: Benchmark embedding methods — compare_embeddings
text: Score registered embedding methods neutral ecological tasks one fair protocol: plot-level train/test split, one scored species-pair set per seed, never-seen negatives link prediction, single presence-mean community definition shared across methods. Co-occurrence metrics need x; EUNIS trait metrics activate labels trait table supplied. species set filtered (min_occurrence) splitting, every method sees species. Runs user dataset, just reference data tuned package default.

title: Benchmark embedding methods — compare_embeddings
code: compare_embeddings(   x,   methods = c("ca", "pmi", "abund_pmi", "glove"),   metrics = c("cooc_ppmi", "cooc_raw", "link_auc", "eunis", "trait"),   dim = 64L,   seeds = 1:3,   test_frac = 0.2,   min_occurrence = 5L,   min_cooccurrence = 1L,   labels = NULL,   traits = NULL,   reference = "ca",   glove_iter = 20L,   control = .benchmark_control() )  specvec_benchmark(   x,   methods = c("ca", "pmi", "abund_pmi", "glove"),   metrics = c("cooc_ppmi", "cooc_raw", "link_auc", "eunis", "trait"),   dim = 64L,   seeds = 1:3,   test_frac = 0.2,   min_occurrence = 5L,   min_cooccurrence = 1L,   labels = NULL,   traits = NULL,   reference = "ca",   glove_iter = 20L,   control = .benchmark_control() )

title: Benchmark embedding methods — compare_embeddings
text: x specvec_data object. methods Method names compare (see specvec_methods()). "glove" skipped note text2vec installed. metrics metrics report: "cooc_ppmi", "cooc_raw", "link_auc", "eunis", "trait". EUNIS/trait skip cleanly inputs absent. dim Embedding dimension. seeds Integer seeds; gives independent split. Reported across-seed mean SD. test_frac Held-plot fraction (species fraction traits). min_occurrence, min_cooccurrence Species/pair filters, applied globally splitting. labels Plot-level label EUNIS metric: column name x$labels, plot-ordered vector, NULL use first label column. traits Species-level numeric attributes trait metric: matrix data frame species row names, NULL skip. reference Method verdict compares (default "ca"). glove_iter Iterations GloVe factorizer. control Scale guards kNN neighbourhood; see defaults source. Override large data.

title: Benchmark embedding methods — compare_embeddings
text: specvec_benchmark: tidy method x metric summary (mean, sd, n), per-seed raw rows, run config, fixed-criterion verdict. method "beats" reference exceeds neutral metrics (cooc_raw link_auc) two pooled SDs.

title: Benchmark embedding methods — compare_embeddings
code: df <- data.frame(   plot = rep(paste0("p", 1:40), each = 3),   species = sample(paste0("s", 1:15), 120, replace = TRUE),   cover = round(runif(120, 1, 100), 1) ) x <- specvec(df, "plot", "species", abundance = "cover") # \donttest{ compare_embeddings(x, methods = c("ca", "pmi", "abund_pmi"),                    dim = 4, seeds = 1:2, min_occurrence = 1) # }

title: Co-occurrence operator (engine primitive) — cooc_matrix
text: Build weighted species x species operator factorization consume (chi_square, return plot x species contingency, whose residual applied implicitly factorization). Exported power users; callers use species_embedding().

title: Co-occurrence operator (engine primitive) — cooc_matrix
code: cooc_matrix(   x,   weighting = c("counts", "ppmi", "abundance_pmi", "chi_square"),   min_occurrence = 5L,   min_cooccurrence = 1L,   time = NULL )

title: Co-occurrence operator (engine primitive) — cooc_matrix
text: x specvec_data object. weighting One "counts", "ppmi", "abundance_pmi", "chi_square". min_occurrence Drop species occurring fewer many plots. min_cooccurrence Keep species pair co-occurs least many plots (species x species weightings ). time Optional time window: restrict plots whose stored time falls window building operator. Numeric time selects closed interval [min(time), max(time)]; otherwise set membership. NULL uses plots.

title: Co-occurrence operator (engine primitive) — cooc_matrix
text: sparse matrix: species x species counts/ppmi/ abundance_pmi, plot x species chi_square.

title: Co-occurrence operator (engine primitive) — cooc_matrix
code: df <- data.frame(plot = c("p1","p1","p2","p2","p3","p3"),                  species = c("A","B","A","B","A","C")) x <- specvec(df, "plot", "species") cooc_matrix(x, "ppmi", min_occurrence = 1)

title: Convert an abundance column to cover-proportion — cover_from_scale
text: x Abundance values: numeric percent, numeric proportion, ordinal cover-abundance codes. scale One "percent", "proportion", "braun_blanquet", registered scale. mapping Optional named numeric vector overriding default lookup (used ordinal scales Braun-Blanquet).

title: Convert an abundance column to cover-proportion — cover_from_scale
text: Numeric cover-proportion [0,1], NA missing unrecognized.

title: Convert an abundance column to cover-proportion — cover_from_scale
code: cover_from_scale(c(0, 50, 100), scale = "percent") cover_from_scale(c("r", "+", "2", "5"), scale = "braun_blanquet")

title: Nearest species — nearest_species
text: Rank species closest focal species embedding. sticky demo function: ask species embedding places next Robinia.

title: Nearest species — nearest_species
code: nearest_species(emb, species, n = 10L, metric = c("cosine", "euclidean"))

title: Nearest species — nearest_species
text: emb specvec_embedding. species Focal species id (row name embedding). n Number neighbours return. metric "cosine" (default) "euclidean".

title: Nearest species — nearest_species
text: tidy data frame ranked nearest-first, species column similarity column (cosine) distance column (euclidean).

title: Nearest species — nearest_species
code: df <- data.frame(plot = rep(paste0("p", 1:8), each = 2),   species = c("A","B","A","B","A","C","B","C","A","B","B","C","A","C","A","B")) emb <- species_embedding(specvec(df, "plot", "species"),                          method = "pmi", dim = 3, min_occurrence = 1) nearest_species(emb, "A", n = 2)

title: Register a cover scale — register_cover_scale
text: Register cover scale

title: Register a cover scale — register_cover_scale
code: register_cover_scale(name, fn)

title: Register a cover scale — register_cover_scale
text: name Scale name. fn Function (x, mapping) returning cover-proportion [0,1], NA missing unrecognized values (imputed downstream).

title: Register a cover scale — register_cover_scale

title: Register a factorization — register_factorization
text: Register factorization

title: Register a factorization — register_factorization
code: register_factorization(   name,   fn,   kind = c("matrix", "objective"),   accepts = c("sym", "implicit", "counts") )

title: Register a factorization — register_factorization
text: name Factorization name. fn Factorization function (operator, dim, ...) returning species x dim matrix species row names. kind "matrix" (svd/eigen; composes symmetric weighting) "objective" (brings loss subsumes weighting). accepts Operator kind factorization consumes ("sym", "implicit", "counts").

title: Register a factorization — register_factorization
text: Invisibly NULL; called side effect registration.

title: Register a method preset — register_method
text: Maps friendly method name (weighting, factorization) pair plus capability descriptor used dispatch validation.

title: Register a method preset — register_method
code: register_method(   name,   weighting,   factorization,   input = c("species_species", "plot_species"),   native_output = c("species", "species_and_community"),   supports_abundance = FALSE,   default_dim = 64L )

title: Register a method preset — register_method
text: name Method name (e.g. "abund_pmi"). weighting Registered weighting name. factorization Registered factorization name. input "species_species" "plot_species". native_output "species" "species_and_community". supports_abundance Logical. default_dim Default embedding dimension.

title: Register a method preset — register_method
text: Invisibly NULL; called side effect registration.

title: Register a weighting — register_weighting
text: weighting builds operator factorization consumes. fn takes (data, kept_species, n_plots, min_cooccurrence) returns operator list kind field ("sym", "counts", "implicit").

title: Register a weighting — register_weighting
code: register_weighting(   name,   fn,   input = c("species_species", "plot_species"),   supports_abundance = FALSE )

title: Register a weighting — register_weighting
text: name Weighting name. fn Operator-building function. input "species_species" "plot_species". supports_abundance Logical; whether weighting uses cover.

title: Register a weighting — register_weighting
text: Invisibly NULL; called side effect registration.

title: Species embedding — species_embedding
text: Learn species vectors co-occurrence. method sugar registered (weighting, factorization) pair; pass weighting/factorization override power use. default "abund_pmi" abundance-weighted PMI.

title: Species embedding — species_embedding
code: species_embedding(   x,   method = "abund_pmi",   dim = 64L,   weighting = NULL,   factorization = NULL,   time = NULL,   min_occurrence = 5L,   min_cooccurrence = 1L,   glove_iter = 20L )

title: Species embedding — species_embedding
text: x specvec_data object. method One specvec_methods(); default "abund_pmi". Pass NULL drive engine purely weighting + factorization. dim Embedding dimension. weighting Optional weighting name overriding method's. factorization Optional factorization name overriding method's. time Optional time window: fit plots whose stored time falls window. Numeric time selects closed interval [min(time), max(time)]; otherwise set membership. NULL (default) uses plots. trajectories comparable across windows, use species_trajectory(). min_occurrence Drop species occurring fewer many plots. min_cooccurrence Keep species pair co-occurs least many plots. glove_iter Iterations GloVe factorizer.

title: Species embedding — species_embedding
text: specvec_embedding: species x dim matrix V (species row names) plus method, capability, preprocessing metadata.

title: Species embedding — species_embedding
code: df <- data.frame(plot = rep(paste0("p", 1:6), each = 2),                  species = c("A","B","A","B","A","C","B","C","A","B","B","C")) x <- specvec(df, "plot", "species") species_embedding(x, method = "pmi", dim = 2, min_occurrence = 1)

title: Species similarity — species_similarity
text: Similarity one species another, species.

title: Species similarity — species_similarity
code: species_similarity(emb, a, b = NULL, metric = c("cosine", "euclidean"))

title: Species similarity — species_similarity
text: emb specvec_embedding. Focal species id. b Optional second species id; NULL, returns named similarity (cosine) distance (euclidean) every species. metric "cosine" (default) "euclidean".

title: Species similarity — species_similarity
text: scalar b supplied, otherwise named numeric vector.

what: Species trajectory through a fixed embedding frame — species_trajectory
title: Species trajectory through a fixed embedding frame — species_trajectory
text: Track focal species time without cross-time alignment problem. single frame embedding fitted (stable coordinate system), focal species placed, per time window, cover-weighted centroid frame species co-occurs window. every window read one fixed frame, trajectory points directly comparable across time – per-window rotation, Procrustes alignment, circularity. engine behind alien-integration trajectories (v0.3).

title: Species trajectory through a fixed embedding frame — species_trajectory
code: species_trajectory(   x,   species,   frame = NULL,   by = NULL,   method = "abund_pmi",   dim = 64L,   weights = c("cover", "presence"),   min_occurrence = 5L,   min_cooccurrence = 1L,   frame_embedding = NULL,   ... )

title: Species trajectory through a fixed embedding frame — species_trajectory
text: x specvec_data object built time column. species Character vector focal species ids trace. frame Optional character vector species defining fixed frame; default every species except species. Window definition passed time splitter: NULL (default) one window per distinct time value, numeric break vector bin time. method Frame embedding method (see specvec_methods()). dim Frame embedding dimension. weights "cover" (default cover present) weights co-occurrence geometric mean covers, matching AbundPMI; "presence" counts co-occurring plots. min_occurrence, min_cooccurrence Frame species/pair filters. frame_embedding Optional pre-fitted specvec_embedding reuse frame instead fitting one (species become frame). ... Passed species_embedding() fitting frame.

title: Species trajectory through a fixed embedding frame — species_trajectory
text: specvec_trajectory: U (focal x window x dim array, NA focal species co-occurrence window), support (focal x window count window plots containing focal species), fixed frame embedding, windows table, pooling provenance.

title: Species trajectory through a fixed embedding frame — species_trajectory
text: frame defaults every species except focal ones, focal species move background community rather helping define . Pass frame fix frame reference set (e.g. native species ).

title: Species trajectory through a fixed embedding frame — species_trajectory
code: set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   plot = rep(paste0("p", 1:60), each = 3),   species = sample(c("focal", paste0("s", 1:12)), 180, replace = TRUE),   decade = rep(c(1990, 2000, 2010), each = 60) ) x <- specvec(df, "plot", "species", time = "decade") tr <- species_trajectory(x, species = "focal", dim = 4, min_occurrence = 1) as.data.frame(tr)

what: specvec: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings — specvec-package
title: specvec: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings — specvec-package
text: Learns species community embeddings ecological co-occurrence data abundance-aware weighting. Turns long 'plot x species (x abundance)' table species vectors first-class community vectors, ships benchmark harness scores embedding methods (correspondence analysis, pointwise mutual information, abundance-weighted PMI, GloVe) neutral ecological tasks. method registered '(weighting, factorization)' pair, new methods added registration rather copying code.

title: specvec: Abundance-Aware Species and Community Embeddings — specvec-package
text: Maintainer: Gilles Colling gilles.colling051@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Gilles Colling gilles.colling051@gmail.com (ORCID)

what: Build a specvec data object — specvec
title: Build a specvec data object — specvec
text: Turn long plot, species (, abundance) table sparse matrices embedding engine needs. Column roles passed name. Abundance, time, labels optional. stored time column powers windowed embeddings trajectories (species_trajectory(), community_trajectory()).

title: Build a specvec data object — specvec
code: specvec(   data,   plot,   species,   abundance = NULL,   time = NULL,   labels = NULL,   cover_scale = c("percent", "proportion", "braun_blanquet"),   duplicates = c("max", "sum", "first", "error"),   cover_mapping = NULL )  as_specvec(   data,   plot,   species,   abundance = NULL,   time = NULL,   labels = NULL,   cover_scale = c("percent", "proportion", "braun_blanquet"),   duplicates = c("max", "sum", "first", "error"),   cover_mapping = NULL )

title: Build a specvec data object — specvec
text: data data frame long form (one row per plot-species record). plot Name plot/site id column. species Name species column. abundance Optional name cover/abundance column. time Optional name plot-level time column (e.g. decade year), stored windowed embeddings trajectories. labels Optional character vector plot-level label column names (carried benchmark; core embedding never needs ). cover_scale read abundance: "percent" (default), "proportion", "braun_blanquet". See cover_from_scale(). duplicates aggregate duplicated plot x species rows: "max" (default), "sum", "first", "error". cover_mapping Optional named numeric vector overriding ordinal cover-scale lookup.

title: Build a specvec data object — specvec
text: specvec_data object: sparse P (presence), COV (cover NULL), sorted species/plots id maps, optional time/labels, meta.

title: Build a specvec data object — specvec
code: df <- data.frame(   plot = c("p1","p1","p2","p2","p3","p3"),   species = c("A","B","A","B","A","C"),   cover = c(40, 10, 60, 5, 30, 80) ) specvec(df, plot = "plot", species = "species", abundance = "cover")

title: specvec 0.2.0
text: Temporal embeddings: track species communities move time.

title: specvec 0.2.0
text: species_trajectory() traces focal species fixed embedding frame. One frame embedding fitted , focal species placed per time window cover-weighted centroid species co-occurs . Every window shares one frame, points compare directly across time without alignment rotation. output carries per-cell support count, thin windows visible rather hidden. community_trajectory() embeds window’s communities shared frame reports per-window novelty reference window, temporal counterpart community_novelty().

title: specvec 0.2.0
text: species_embedding(), community_embedding(), cooc_matrix() gain time = argument fits plots time window. reserved time seam 0.1 now live.

title: specvec 0.1.0
text: First public release.

title: specvec 0.1.0
text: specvec() builds specvec object long plot-species table. Abundance optional read percent, proportion, Braun-Blanquet scale (cover_scale =). species_embedding() learns species vectors co-occurrence. default abund_pmi method abundance-weighted PMI; ca, pmi, glove available method registry. community_embedding() places plot species space pooling species vectors, weighted cover presence. nearest_species(), species_similarity(), community_similarity(), community_novelty() query learned space.

title: specvec 0.1.0
text: compare_embeddings() scores methods data one fair protocol: held-co-occurrence recovery, link prediction, optional habitat (EUNIS) trait recovery. reports method wins two pooled standard deviations.

title: specvec 0.1.0
text: method registered (weighting, factorization) pair. register_weighting(), register_factorization(), register_method() add new methods, specvec_methods() lists registered.

title: specvec 0.1.0
text: vignette("specvec-methods") derives co-occurrence operator, AbundPMI formula, baselines. vignette("specvec-benchmark") runs compare_embeddings() simulated data EVA export.
